title: Physiotherapist
description: Physiotherapists help patients recover from injuries, illnesses, or disabilities through movement and exercise, manual therapy, education, and advice. They work with people of all ages to improve their quality of life.
content: Physiotherapists assess, diagnose, and treat patients with musculoskeletal, neurological, and cardiorespiratory conditions. They develop individualized treatment plans, educate patients on self-management strategies, and promote overall health and well-being. In India, they work in hospitals, clinics, rehabilitation centers, sports teams, and private practices.

financialFeasibility.note: A bachelor's or master's degree in physiotherapy is required, which can be expensive. Loan options and scholarships are available. Starting salaries are moderate but increase with experience.

sectorScore.remarks: Opportunities exist in both government and private sectors in India. Private hospitals and clinics often offer better salaries and growth prospects.

relatedCareers.reason: Similar focus on rehabilitation and improving daily living skills.
relatedCareers.title: Occupational Therapist

relatedCareers.reason: Specializes in treating sports-related injuries and enhancing athletic performance.
relatedCareers.title: Sports Therapist

relatedCareers.reason: Provides support and guidance to individuals with disabilities to achieve their personal and vocational goals.
relatedCareers.title: Rehabilitation Counselor

genderDistribution.note: Physiotherapy is becoming increasingly popular among women in India, but male practitioners are still more common.
